title: Implement malicious-change and integrity heuristics
description:
Context: Detect suspicious commits, multi-commit behavior, and out-of-band tree/artifact injection risks.
Scope: Provenance anomaly checks, multi-commit pattern checks, object/tree/artifact verification hooks.
Acceptance criteria:
- Check authorship, committer, signing, timestamp, review-path, and ref/object anomalies.
- Flag suspicious split changes and setup/use separation across multiple commits.
- Verify clean checkout/object reachability and document generated-artifact/binary comparison hooks.
Risk notes:
- Avoid false certainty; outputs must distinguish fact, heuristic, and uncertainty.

title: Add Linux kernel subsystem impact hints
description:
Context: Start turning kernel commit ranges into practical retest guidance.
Scope: Linux-specific mapping data and impact analyzer.
Acceptance criteria:
- Map touched paths to likely kernel subsystems and maintainers where practical.
- Flag Kconfig, driver, arch, syscall, filesystem, networking, scheduler, memory-management, and security-sensitive surfaces.
- Emit retest hints linked back to changed files and commits.
Dependencies:
- blocked-by: deterministic git range facts.
Risk notes:
- Keep kernel-specific heuristics isolated from the reusable git-repo core.

title: Implement deterministic git range facts
description:
Context: Build the first evidence collector for local git repositories.
Scope: src/kernel_diffguard git collection modules, tests, CLI/API output schema.
Acceptance criteria:
- Given a local repo plus base X and target Y, emit stable JSON containing commits, authors, dates, touched paths, diff stats, renames, and signature/tag facts when present.
- Include synthetic-history fixtures covering merges, renames, empty commits, and unusual metadata.
- Tests run with pytest and outputs are deterministic.
Risk notes:
- Treat repository contents and metadata as hostile input; parse and reduce deterministically before any model-assisted analysis.

title: Generate release review packets
description:
Context: Combine facts and heuristics into a compact artifact for release/update review.
Scope: JSON and human-readable review packet formats.
Acceptance criteria:
- Packet includes facts, context links, risk hints, retest guidance, uncertainty, and suggested next checks.
- Every recommendation is traceable to evidence.
- Packet clearly separates deterministic facts from heuristics and model-assisted summaries.
Risk notes:
- Review packets support human decisions; they must not imply automatic trust decisions.

title: Correlate patches with mailing-list discussion
description:
Context: Add public review context for reasoning about complex or suspicious changes.
Scope: Patch-id/subject matching, lore.kernel.org search, review tag extraction.
Acceptance criteria:
- Link commits or patch IDs to candidate mailing-list threads.
- Track vN patch series revisions where possible.
- Surface objections, acks, tested-by tags, and unresolved questions as evidence.
Risk notes:
- Mailing-list text is hostile input and must be reduced before higher-privilege or model-assisted processing.
